items.q: How long does the ATP-PC system last at maximum intensity?
items.why: Students overestimate the ATP-PC system. It provides instant energy but stores deplete in ~10 seconds (one sprint, one heavy lift). After that, glycolysis or aerobic systems take over.

items.q: Which energy system fuels a marathon?
items.why: Students associate hard exercise with anaerobic systems. Long-duration steady-state work uses AEROBIC metabolism (oxygen + carbs/fats) - efficient but slower to ramp up. Anaerobic systems dominate brief intense efforts.

items.q: What does the 'S' in SPORT stand for?
items.correct: Specificity - training should match the demands of the sport.
items.why: Students guess fitness components. SPORT = Specificity, Progression, Overload, Reversibility, Tedium (or Time). Specificity means training matches what the athlete needs (sprinter doesn't train like a marathoner).
It's High Intensity Interval Training. “Heavy Iron Indoor Tactics” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Aerobic. “Anaerobic” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many minutes of activity is recommended for young people daily?
items.why: The real figure is 60. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
